title: Set Up Your Lathe
intro: Before you can turn, the lathe needs mounting up. Grab each piece from the toolbox on the bench and fit it where it belongs.

steps.instruction: Seat the spur drive centre in the headstock spindle.
steps.why: Its four prongs bite into the end of the blank so the headstock can drive it round.

steps.instruction: Slot the live centre into the tailstock.
steps.why: A spinning point that supports the far end of the blank between centres.

steps.instruction: Clamp the tool rest onto the bed, just in front of the blank.
steps.why: The bar your tool rides on — set it close to the work so the tool is well supported.

steps.instruction: Plug the lathe into the wall outlet.
steps.why: No power, no spin. Always plug in last, after everything is mounted.

decoys.wrongReason: A faceplate screws onto the spindle thread for bowls and platters — not for between-centres spindle work.
A scroll chuck grips a tenon for bowl work — you don't need it to turn between centres.
A drill chuck holds bits to bore down the centre — it isn't part of mounting a spindle blank.
